scheme: 3.  Geometric MULTIPLIES by r; arithmetic ADDS d

scheme: 7.  Step 1  test small primes:; Step 2  2 is prime, but 2 is even.; Step 3  so not every prime is odd.; The single example n = 2 disproves the statement.  ∎
10.  6 is a multiple of 3 but even

scheme: 7.  Inside (x − 3): horizontal shift RIGHT 3; Outside (+ 2): vertical shift UP 2; So: translate 3 right and 2 up.
8.  2 f(x): vertical stretch, scale factor 2; − 1: translate DOWN 1; So: stretch ×2 vertically, then move down 1.

16.  (a) Translation 2 left  (A1); translation 3 down  (A1)(A1)
(b) x: 1 → −1, y: 4 → 1  (M1) → (−1, 1)  (A1)

questions.prompt: Which is more spread out: {5,5,5} or {1,5,9}?

mean 8 and σ ≈ 2.97. What does σ tell us?

Class B: mean 62, σ = 11. Compare the two classes.

(b) Find the standard deviation.   [2]

scheme: 2.  how far values are from the centre
7.  Mean x̄ = 8 (the centre).; σ ≈ 2.97 → on average values lie about 3 units from the mean.; A small σ would mean the data is tightly packed.
8.  Same mean → same average score.; Class A has the smaller σ (4 < 11).; So Class A is more consistent; B's results vary much more.
12.  better on average but less consistent

scheme: 4.  e.g. modelling a sound wave with sine functions
5.  e.g. optimising a route / cost with functions
6.  it's just retelling theory — little personal maths
7.  Broad interest: football.; Narrow it: 'Modelling the flight of a free kick.'; Focus it with maths: use a quadratic / projectile model; and compare to real shot data.
8.  No — far too broad to explore in depth.; Better: pick ONE context, e.g.; 'Which function best models my city's monthly rainfall?'; One focused question, real data, clear analysis.
10.  e.g. modelling a basketball shot's arc

title: Worksheet — Lesson 45: Structuring the IA to the Criteria

questions.prompt: How many assessment criteria are there?
Using correct ∫ and Σ notation → which?
Where do clear graphs and correct notation earn marks?

Which is reflection?
(a) 'The graph goes up.'
(b) 'The model fits early data but fails later — likely because growth can't continue forever.'

An IA uses correct mathematics but reads like a textbook chapter, with no personal voice.

(a) Which TWO criteria will score poorly?   [2]
(b) Suggest one change to improve each.   [2]

scheme: 7.  Clear, labelled graphs and layout → Criterion A.; Correct symbols and terminology → Criterion B.; So presentation AND notation both score —; polish them, don't leave them to chance.
8.  (a) is description — it just states what happened.; (b) is reflection — it interprets and questions the result.; Aim for (b)-style comments throughout.

16.  (a) C (personal engagement) and D (reflection)  (A1)(A1)
(b) C: add why you chose it / your own data; D: add critical comments on results & limitations  (A1)(A1)
